text: The surgeon ties the purse string and performs the first wall shot of the rectal stump, then proceeds with circumferential full thickness rectotomy, freeing the rectal wall to facilitate further dissection.
The surgeon performs a double continuous suture using a V-lock suture to reinforce the purse string on the rectal stump following the circumferential full thickness rectotomy.
The surgeon achieves complete inversion of the rectal mucosa, creating a superior airtight seal at the rectal stump.
The surgeon performs a second generous washout of the pelvic area before proceeding with further dissection.
The surgeon performs a down-to-up dissection through the total mesorectal excision (TME) plane, carefully separating the rectal tissue layers.
